increase post-thaw viability of HeLa ce
questions.question:
Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will
increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared
to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose’s superior membrane stabilization at
low temperatures.
questions.objective: Confusion in experimental design: states 'replacing sucrose' but compares to 'DMSO protocol' - clarify baseline condition; Missing key protocol details: freezing rate, storage duration, thaw procedure; No specification of viability measurement method; Trehalose typically requires special delivery methods (not membrane permeable like DMSO) - approach not stated

questions.title: Supplementing C57BL/6 mice with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG for 4 weeks will reduce intestinal permeability by at least 3
questions.question: Supplementing C57BL/6 mice with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG for 4 weeks will reduce intestinal permeability by at least 30% compared to controls, measured by FITC-dextran assay, due to upregulation of tight junction proteins claudin-1 and occludin.
questions.objective: Control group implied but composition not specified (standard diet vs vehicle); Missing probiotic dose and delivery method; No sample size or statistical power consideration; Tight junction measurement method unclear - will this be verified by Western blot or immunohistochemistry?

In conclusion, with wild type S. ovata as the microbial catalyst and at a cathode potential of −690 mV versus SHE, MES system equipped with RGO paper cathode is more performant than MES systems equipped with other types of freestanding carbonaceous cathode including carbon paper (Table 1). Besides higher acetate production rate and current density (Table 1), RGO paper cathode also has better flexibility that make it possible to be folded in the MES reactor to increase its surface area and thus improve electron exchange with microbial catalysts. Further experiments are warranted to find novel RGO paper electrode conformation that may increase the productivity of MES towards practical application.

previews.validationJson: {"validationApproach":"The primary endpoint (≥15 percentage point increase in post-thaw viability with trehalose vs. DMSO) will be validated through two independent methods: (1) Trypan blue exclusion assay measuring membrane integrity at immediate post-thaw and 24h time points, and (2) MTT metabolic activity assay at 24h post-thaw measuring functional cell viability. Success requires trehalose group to demonstrate statistically significant (p<0.05, one-way ANOVA with Tukey post-hoc) viability improvement of ≥15 absolute percentage points compared to 10% DMSO control at the 24h time point. For example, if DMSO control shows 75% viability, trehalose must achieve ≥90% viability. Secondary validation includes morphological assessment by phase contrast microscopy to confirm normal HeLa cell morphology and attachment patterns. The 24h time point is critical as it reflects true recovery capacity beyond immediate membrane integrity, aligning with published protocols showing that post-thaw culture and 24h measurement are crucial for evaluating cryoprotectant efficacy.","totalBudgetEstimate":5623,"currency":"GBP"}

blue exclusion assay measuring membrane integrity at immediate post-thaw and 24h time points, and (2) MTT metabolic activity assay at 24h post-thaw measuring functional cell viability. Success requires trehalose group to demonstrate statistically significant (p<0.05, one-way ANOVA with Tukey post-hoc) viability improvement of ≥15 absolute percentage points compared to 10% DMSO control at the 24h time point. previews.criticSummaryJson: {"confidenceScore":78,"approved":false,"warnings":["Extracellular trehalose (0.2 M) without membrane permeabilization has limited intracellular access — published protocols typically use cell-permeabilizing methods or trehalose-loaded liposomes for mammalian cells","MTT final concentration (0.5 mg/mL) and 4-hour incubation is appropriate, but formazan dissolution in DMSO should include 10-15 min incubation with agitation for complete solubilization","Liquid nitrogen cost (£180 for 100L) appears low for UK 2026 — typical pricing £250-350 for this volume including delivery and dewars rental","No mention of positive control (freshly cultured cells without freezing) to establish baseline viability for HeLa cells at passage 5-15","Statistical power calculation absent — n=3 biological replicates may be underpowered to detect exactly 15 percentage point difference with typical viability assay CV of 5-8%","Trehalose membrane impermeability issue: HeLa cells lack trehalose transporters, so extracellular trehalose primarily stabilizes outer membrane leaflet — hypothesis claims may overstate mechanism"],"blockers":["Critical mechanistic gap: Protocol does NOT address trehalose cell membrane permeability — extracellular 0.2 M trehalose without permeabilization (electroporation, engineered pores, or CPP-conjugation) will NOT achieve significant intracellular concentrations. Published reference PMC6382607 explicitly discusses intracellular delivery methods. Current protocol may show minimal effect compared to hypothesis claim"],"summary":"Operationally sound protocol with appropriate reagent concentrations, realistic timeline, and dual viability validation, but contains a fundamental flaw: extracellular trehalose application without membrane permeabilization contradicts the stated mechanism and cited literature on intracellular cryoprotection."}

previews.title: LGG Probiotic Supplementation Effects on Intestinal Barrier in C57BL/6 Mice
previews.summary: This in vivo study evaluates whether 4-week oral Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG supplementation reduces intestinal permeability by ≥30% in C57BL/6 mice compared to controls, measured via FITC-dextran assay. The mechanism is assessed by quantifying tight junction protein expression (claudin-1, occludin) in intestinal epithelium using Western blot and immunofluorescence.
previews.objective: Determine if probiotic supplementation reduces intestinal permeability in mice
previews.hypothesis: Supplementing C57BL/6 mice with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG for 4 weeks will reduce intestinal permeability by at least 30% compared to controls, measured by FITC-dextran assay, due to upregulation of tight junction proteins claudin-1 and occludin.
previews.protocolJson: [{"stepNumber":1,"title":"Animal Procurement and Acclimatization","description":"Obtain 24 male C57BL/6 mice (8-10 weeks, 20-25g) from Charles River UK and house in SPF conditions (12h light/dark, 22±2°C, 50-60% humidity) with ad libitum access to standard chow and water. Acclimatize for 1 week before intervention.","duration":"1 week","sourceProtocol":"Standard rodent husbandry protocol - Charles River","sourceUrl":"https://www.criver.com/products-services/find-model/c57bl6n-mouse","criticalNote":"Ensure Home Office Project License compliance and ethical approval before procurement"},{"stepNumber":2,"title":"Probiotic Preparation and Gavage Administration","description":"Reconstitute Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG ATCC 53103 in MRS broth to 1×10^9 CFU/ml and administer 200μl daily via oral gavage to treatment group (n=12) for 4 weeks. Control group (n=12) receives vehicle (MRS broth) only.","duration":"4 weeks daily","sourceProtocol":"Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG gavage protocol - PMC3404581","sourceUrl":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3404581/criticalNote","criticalNote":"Prepare fresh bacterial suspension weekly and verify CFU by plating on MRS agar"},{"stepNumber":3,"title":"FITC-Dextran Intestinal Permeability Assay","description":"After 4-week intervention, fast mice for 4h then gavage with FITC-dextran (4kDa, 600mg/kg body weight in PBS). Collect blood via cardiac puncture after 4h under isoflurane anesthesia and measure plasma FITC fluorescence (excitation 485nm, emission 528nm) using plate reader.","duration":"6 hours","sourceProtocol":"FITC-dextran permeability assay - Nature Protocols","sourceUrl":"https://www.nature.com/articles/nprot.2012.111","criticalNote":"Protect samples from light and process immediately to prevent fluorescence quenching"},{"stepNumber":4,"title":"Tissue Collection and Processing","description":"Following blood collection, euthanize mice via cervical dislocation and harvest proximal small intestine (jejunum, 5cm segment). Divide tissue: half snap-frozen in liquid nitrogen for protein analysis, half fixed in 4% paraformaldehyde for immunofluorescence.","duration":"30 min per mouse","sourceProtocol":"Intestinal tissue harvesting protocol - Bio-protocol","sourceUrl":"https://bio-protocol.org/e1039","criticalNote":"Work rapidly on ice to preserve protein integrity"},{"stepNumber":5,"title":"Protein Extraction and Western Blot Analysis","description":"Homogenize frozen intestinal tissue in RIPA buffer with protease inhibitors, quantify protein by BCA assay, and perform Western blot for claudin-1 (23kDa), occludin (65kDa), and β-actin loading control. Use primary antibodies overnight at 4°C, HRP-conjugated secondaries for 1h at room temperature, and ECL detection.","duration":"2 days","sourceProtocol":"Tight junction protein Western blot - protocols.io","sourceUrl":"https://www.protocols.io/view/western-blot-protocol-for-tight-junction-proteins-bp2l6bzrgqe5/v1","criticalNote":"Optimize antibody dilutions in pilot experiments before full cohort analysis"},{"stepNumber":6,"title":"Immunofluorescence Staining of Tight Junction Proteins","description":"Section paraffin-embedded jejunum at 5μm, deparaffinize, perform antigen retrieval (citrate buffer pH 6.0, 95°C, 20min), block with 5% BSA, and incubate with anti-claudin-1 and anti-occludin antibodies overnight at 4°C. Apply Alexa Fluor-conjugated secondaries and mount with DAPI for confocal imaging.","duration":"3 days","sourceProtocol":"Immunofluorescence protocol for intestinal sections - JOVE","sourceUrl":"https://www.jove.com/t/54647/immunofluorescence-staining-intestinal-stem-cells","criticalNote":"Include negative controls with secondary antibody only"},{"stepNumber":7,"title":"Quantitative Image Analysis","description":"Acquire confocal images at 63× magnification (minimum 10 fields per mouse) and quantify claudin-1 and occludin fluorescence intensity using ImageJ/Fiji. Measure integrated density along villus epithelium and normalize to DAPI-stained nuclei for standardization across samples.","duration":"1 week","sourceProtocol":"Fluorescence quantification protocol - protocols.io","sourceUrl":"https://www.protocols.io/view/quantification-of-immunofluorescence-images-using-bwk2pcye","criticalNote":"Maintain consistent microscope settings across all samples"},{"stepNumber":8,"title":"Statistical Analysis","description":"Calculate mean FITC-dextran plasma concentration and tight junction protein expression for each group. Perform unpaired t-tests or Mann-Whitney U tests to compare treatment vs control, using GraphPad Prism 10. Consider p<0.05 significant and calculate percent reduction in permeability.","duration":"3 days","sourceProtocol":"Statistical methods for in vivo studies - Nature Methods","sourceUrl":"https://www.nature.com/articles/nmeth.2738","criticalNote":"Verify data normality before selecting parametric vs non-parametric tests"}]

previews.validationJson: {"validationApproach":"The primary endpoint (≥30% reduction in intestinal permeability) is validated by comparing plasma FITC-dextran concentrations between LGG-treated and control groups using unpaired t-test, with successful validation requiring treatment group values at least 30% lower than controls (p<0.05). The mechanistic hypothesis is tested by Western blot quantification of claudin-1 and occludin expression in jejunal epithelium, requiring statistically significant upregulation in LGG-treated mice compared to controls, supported by immunofluorescence localization showing enhanced tight junction protein staining at epithelial cell borders. A successful outcome requires both functional (FITC-dextran) and molecular (tight junction proteins) endpoints to demonstrate concordance between reduced permeability and increased barrier protein expression. Power analysis indicates n=12 per group provides 80% power to detect 30% difference at α=0.05 assuming 15% coefficient of variation.","totalBudgetEstimate":6006,"currency":"GBP"}

previews.title: Bioelectrochemical CO₂ Fixation with Sporomusa ovata at −400mV Cathode Potential
previews.summary: This experiment evaluates acetate production from CO₂ using Sporomusa ovata in a bioelectrochemical system with cathode potential at −400mV vs SHE. Target is ≥150 mmol/L/day acetate, representing ≥20% improvement over current benchmarks.
previews.objective: Demonstrate enhanced CO₂ fixation into acetate using Sporomusa ovata in a bioelectrochemical system
previews.hypothesis: Introducing Sporomusa ovata into a bioelectrochemical system at a cathode potential of −400mV vs SHE will fix CO₂ into acetate at a rate of at least 150 mmol/L/day, outperforming current biocatalytic carbon capture benchmarks by at least 20%.
previews.protocolJson: [{"stepNumber":1,"title":"Culture Sporomusa ovata and prepare inoculum","description":"Grow S. ovata anaerobically in modified DSM 311 medium with 80% H₂/20% CO₂ headspace at 30°C until mid-log phase (OD600 0.4-0.6). Harvest cells by centrifugation (5000g, 10 min) and resuspend in anaerobic medium to OD600 1.0 for inoculation.","duration":"3-4 days","sourceProtocol":"Nature Scientific Reports - S. ovata culture protocol","sourceUrl":"https://www.nature.com/articles/srep16168","criticalNote":"Maintain strict anaerobic conditions throughout"},{"stepNumber":2,"title":"Assemble bioelectrochemical reactor with graphite cathode","description":"Construct H-cell reactor with graphite felt cathode (10 cm²) and Pt anode separated by proton exchange membrane. Fill cathode chamber with 100 mL anaerobic bicarbonate buffer (50 mM, pH 7.0) and continuously purge with CO₂.","duration":"2 hours","sourceProtocol":"Nature Communications Chemistry - BES assembly","sourceUrl":"https://www.nature.com/articles/s42004-019-0145-0"},{"stepNumber":3,"title":"Set cathode potential and verify electrochemical stability","description":"Connect to potentiostat and poise cathode at −400mV vs SHE (Ag/AgCl reference electrode). Run baseline cyclic voltammetry to confirm stable potential control and low background current density (<0.5 mA/cm²).","duration":"1 hour","sourceProtocol":"Nature Scientific Reports - electrode characterization","sourceUrl":"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-017-09841-7"},{"stepNumber":4,"title":"Inoculate reactor with Sporomusa ovata","description":"Inject 10 mL prepared S. ovata inoculum (OD600 1.0) into cathode chamber under anaerobic conditions. Maintain cathode potential at −400mV and temperature at 30°C with continuous CO₂ sparging at 20 mL/min.","duration":"30 minutes","sourceProtocol":"PMC article on S. ovata MES","sourceUrl":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9253864/cri"},{"stepNumber":5,"title":"Monitor biofilm formation on cathode surface","description":"Sample cathode chamber liquid (1 mL) daily and quantify planktonic cell density by OD600. Perform scanning electron microscopy of cathode surface at day 3 and 7 to visualize biofilm development and coverage.","duration":"10 days, sampling daily","sourceProtocol":"Nature Scientific Reports - biofilm analysis","sourceUrl":"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-017-09841-7"},{"stepNumber":6,"title":"Quantify acetate production by HPLC","description":"Collect 1 mL samples from cathode chamber every 24h. Analyze acetate concentration by HPLC with refractive index detector (Aminex HPX-87H column, 5 mM H₂SO₄ mobile phase, 0.6 mL/min, 60°C). Calculate production rate as mmol/L/day.","duration":"Daily sampling for 14 days","sourceProtocol":"Nature Communications Chemistry - product analysis","sourceUrl":"https://www.nature.com/articles/s42004-019-0145-0"},{"stepNumber":7,"title":"Measure current density and coulombic efficiency","description":"Record current continuously via potentiostat. Calculate coulombic efficiency as (moles acetate × 8 electrons/mol) / (total charge passed). Target >80% efficiency for robust CO₂ reduction pathway activity.","duration":"Continuous monitoring","sourceProtocol":"PMC extremophile electrosynthesis study","sourceUrl":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7174410/cri"},{"stepNumber":8,"title":"Perform benchmark comparison with control conditions","description":"Run parallel reactors at −600mV and −800mV cathode potentials with identical inoculum. Compare acetate rates to published benchmarks (125 mmol/L/day baseline) to verify ≥20% improvement at −400mV optimized conditions.","duration":"14 days parallel operation","sourceProtocol":"Nature Scientific Reports - comparative MES study","sourceUrl":"https://www.nature.com/articles/srep16168"}]

previews.validationJson: {"validationApproach":"The hypothesis is validated by continuous HPLC quantification of acetate in the cathode chamber over 14 days of operation. Success requires acetate production rate ≥150 mmol/L/day at −400mV cathode potential, measured daily in triplicate. This target represents ≥20% improvement over published baseline of 125 mmol/L/day from literature benchmarks. Parallel control reactors at −600mV and −800mV provide comparative data. Coulombic efficiency calculation confirms electrons flow to acetate via Wood-Ljungdahl pathway rather than side reactions. SEM imaging verifies biofilm formation on cathode surface. Statistical significance assessed by t-test comparing −400mV performance to published benchmarks (p<0.05). Current density monitoring ensures stable electrochemical conditions throughout. If acetate rate falls below 150 mmol/L/day, hypothesis is rejected.","totalBudgetEstimate":9499,"currency":"GBP"}

previews.title: CRISPR-Cas12a Lateral Flow Diagnostic for Point-of-Care Tuberculosis Detection in Unprocessed Sputum
previews.summary: This diagnostic validation study evaluates a lyophilized CRISPR-Cas12a lateral flow assay targeting IS6110 insertion sequence for direct detection of Mycobacterium tuberculosis in unprocessed sputum samples within 45 minutes.
previews.objective: Develop and validate a CRISPR-Cas12a lateral flow assay for rapid tuberculosis detection in unprocessed sputum samples
previews.hypothesis: CRISPR-Cas12a lateral flow assay targeting IS6110 insertion sequence of Mycobacterium tuberculosis in unprocessed sputum samples from patients with active tuberculosis infection will achieve diagnostic sensitivity ≥95%, specificity ≥97%, and detection limit 10 CFU/mL compared to MGIT liquid culture reference standard, with lyophilized reagents stable at 37°C, no cold-chain storage required, 45-minute turnaround time, and direct application to unprocessed sputum, because CRISPR-Cas12a collateral cleavage activity upon target recognition produces visible signal on lateral flow strip
previews.protocolJson: [{"stepNumber":1,"title":"Clinical Sample Collection and Preparation","description":"Collect unprocessed sputum samples (2-5 mL) from 400 patients with suspected active tuberculosis presenting at study sites, including both smear-positive and smear-negative cases. Split each sample for parallel MGIT culture reference testing and CRISPR-Cas12a assay testing, maintaining sample aliquots at 4°C until processing within 24 hours.","duration":"24 hours","sourceProtocol":"WHO Consolidated Guidelines on TB Diagnostics","sourceUrl":"https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK570098/pdf/Bookshelf_NBK570098.pdf","criticalNote":"Unprocessed sputum contains PCR inhibitors; sample handling procedures must prevent cross-contamination between high and low bacterial load specimens"},{"stepNumber":2,"title":"Direct Sputum DNA Extraction Using Heat Lysis","description":"Transfer 100 μL unprocessed sputum to extraction buffer containing 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 8.0), 1 mM EDTA, 0.5% Triton X-100, and heat at 95°C for 15 minutes followed by centrifugation at 10,000g for 2 minutes. Use supernatant directly for amplification without further purification to maintain 45-minute total turnaround time.","duration":"20 minutes","sourceProtocol":"Streamlined Point-of-Care CRISPR Test for TB Detection Directly from Sputum","sourceUrl":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11875272/html","criticalNote":"Heat lysis releases mycobacterial DNA but does not completely eliminate PCR inhibitors; optimization may require addition of BSA or betaine to amplification buffer"},{"stepNumber":3,"title":"Recombinase Polymerase Amplification (RPA) of IS6110 Target","description":"Perform isothermal RPA amplification targeting IS6110 insertion sequence (16-19 copies per MTB genome) using lyophilized primer mix at 37°C for 20 minutes in one-pot reaction. Use asymmetric primer ratios (1:10) to generate single-stranded DNA products optimal for Cas12a detection, following published RPA protocols for mycobacterial targets.","duration":"20 minutes","sourceProtocol":"Streamlined CRISPR-based test for tuberculosis detection","sourceUrl":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12327463/html","criticalNote":"RPA is temperature-sensitive; maintain 37°C ± 2°C using portable heat blocks suitable for field deployment"},{"stepNumber":4,"title":"CRISPR-Cas12a Trans-Cleavage Reaction","description":"Add pre-complexed Cas12a-crRNA targeting IS6110 (final concentration 50 nM Cas12a, 62.5 nM crRNA) and fluorophore-quencher ssDNA reporter probe to RPA product in one-pot format. Incubate at 37°C for 10 minutes to allow trans-cleavage activity to cleave reporter probes upon target recognition.","duration":"10 minutes","sourceProtocol":"CRISPR-Cas12a-based platform for ultrasensitive MTB detection","sourceUrl":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10242030/html","criticalNote":"Cas12a enzyme activity is sensitive to salt concentration and pH; buffer conditions must be optimized for combined RPA-Cas12a compatibility"},{"stepNumber":5,"title":"Lateral Flow Detection and Result Interpretation","description":"Transfer 80 μL reaction mixture to lateral flow strip sample pad and allow capillary flow for 5 minutes. Read visual test line intensity at 5 minutes; positive result shows visible test line from cleaved reporter binding to detection zone, control line must appear in all valid tests.","duration":"5 minutes","sourceProtocol":"Development of CRISPR-Cas12a-based multiplex detection of MTB","sourceUrl":"https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37711452/html","criticalNote":"Lateral flow strips must be stored in sealed foil pouches with desiccant; humidity >60% can cause false positives due to non-specific reporter cleavage"},{"stepNumber":6,"title":"MGIT Liquid Culture Reference Testing","description":"Inoculate 0.5 mL sputum into MGIT tubes containing modified Middlebrook 7H9 broth and PANTA supplement following manufacturer protocol. Incubate in MGIT 960 system at 37°C with automated fluorescence monitoring for up to 42 days or until positive signal detected.","duration":"42 days","sourceProtocol":"MGIT Procedure Manual BD Diagnostics","sourceUrl":"https://www.bd.com/en-us/products-and-solutions/products/product-families/bactec-mgit-960-system","criticalNote":"MGIT culture remains gold standard but requires 10-42 days; contamination rate 3-8% in sputum samples requires repeat testing"},{"stepNumber":7,"title":"Analytical Sensitivity Testing with Spiked Sputum","description":"Spike pooled TB-negative sputum with known concentrations of H37Rv strain (10^5 to 10 CFU/mL) to determine limit of detection (LoD) using probit analysis with minimum 20 replicates per concentration. Test triplicate on three separate days to establish reproducibility at target LoD of 10 CFU/mL.","duration":"3 days","sourceProtocol":"Nature protocols for diagnostic assay validation","sourceUrl":"https://www.nature.com/articles/nprot.2016.065","criticalNote":"CFU enumeration in sputum has 0.5 log variation; use fresh culture dilutions and verify CFU counts by plating on 7H11 agar"},{"stepNumber":8,"title":"Cross-Reactivity Testing with NTM and Other Pathogens","description":"Test assay specificity against panel of 15 non-tuberculous mycobacteria (M. avium, M. intracellulare, M. kansasii, etc.) and 20 common respiratory pathogens at 10^6 CFU/mL to confirm no cross-reactivity. Include M. bovis BCG as positive control for MTB complex detection.","duration":"2 days","sourceProtocol":"CLSI guideline for molecular diagnostic method validation","sourceUrl":"https://clsi.org/standards/products/molecular-diagnostics/documents/mm09/html","criticalNote":"IS6110 is absent in M. bovis BCG vaccine strain and some MTB outbreak strains; consider dual-target assay including IS1081 for comprehensive detection"},{"stepNumber":9,"title":"Lyophilized Reagent Stability Testing at 37°C","description":"Prepare lyophilized RPA-Cas12a reagent pellets using trehalose and mannitol cryoprotectants, store at 37°C in sealed tubes with desiccant for 6 months. Test functional activity monthly against fresh reagents using control templates to confirm ≥90% activity retention throughout storage period.","duration":"180 days","sourceProtocol":"Lyophilization protocols for enzyme preservation","sourceUrl":"https://bio-protocol.org/en/bpdetail?id=4632&type=0","criticalNote":"Cas12a enzyme stability is limiting factor; some formulations show only 70% activity after 3 months at 37°C; consider alternative thermostable Cas12a variants"},{"stepNumber":10,"title":"Clinical Validation and Statistical Analysis","description":"Calculate sensitivity, specificity, positive predictive value, and negative predictive value with 95% confidence intervals using MGIT culture as reference standard. previews.validationJson: {"validationApproach":"The primary validation approach compares CRISPR-Cas12a lateral flow results against MGIT liquid culture as the reference standard in 400 prospectively enrolled patients with suspected active tuberculosis, calculating sensitivity, specificity, positive predictive value, and negative predictive value with 95% confidence intervals. Analytical validation establishes the limit of detection at 10 CFU/mL using H37Rv-spiked sputum samples tested in 20 replicates across multiple days, with probit analysis to determine the concentration at which 95% of replicates are detected. Cross-reactivity testing against 15 non-tuberculous mycobacteria strains and 20 respiratory pathogens at 10^6 CFU/mL confirms zero false positives, validating the specificity target of ≥97%. Lyophilized reagent stability is validated through real-time storage at 37°C for 6 months with monthly functional testing, confirming ≥90% activity retention and meeting the claim of no cold-chain requirement for field deployment.","totalBudgetEstimate":64113,"currency":"GBP"}

manufacturing scale-up"]}],"validationApproach":"The primary validation approach compares CRISPR-Cas12a lateral flow results against MGIT liquid culture as the reference standard in 400 prospectively enrolled patients with suspected active tuberculosis, calculating sensitivity, specificity, positive predictive value, and negative predictive value with 95% confidence intervals. Analytical validation establishes the limit of detection at 10 CFU/mL using H37Rv-spiked sputum samples tested in 20 replicates across multiple days, with probit analysis to determine the concentration at which 95% of replicates are detected. Single-target assays targeting IS6110 will miss these variant strains, failing to achieve 95% sensitivity across diverse TB strain populations.","signalToWatch":"False-negative results in known culture-positive samples; geographic clustering of assay failures; detection failures in Beijing lineage-prevalent regions (East Asia, South Africa).","mitigation":"Implement dual-target assay with IS6110 plus IS1081 (6 copies per genome, present in all MTB complex) or incorporate mpb64 target; clustering of positives in specific collection sites or processing batches; discordance where CRISPR-positive but culture-negative or culture-growing NTM.","mitigation":"Implement strict sample collection protocols using sterile water for mouth rinses; previews.title: Overexpression of Thermophilic Rubisco in Synechocystis PCC 6803 to Enhance Carbon Fixation
previews.summary: This experiment constructs a Synechocystis PCC 6803 strain overexpressing T. elongatus rbcL under Ptrc promoter and measures carbon fixation via ¹⁴C-bicarbonate incorporation and oxygen evolution under controlled conditions. The primary endpoint is at least 25% increase in carbon fixation rate compared to wild-type, validated by western blot confirmation of rbcL expression.
previews.objective: Enhance carbon fixation rate in Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803 through heterologous expression of thermophilic rubisco large subunit
previews.hypothesis: Overexpression of Thermosynechococcus elongatus rbcL (large subunit of rubisco) under constitutive Ptrc promoter in Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803 will increase carbon fixation rate by at least 25% compared to wild-type under continuous illumination at 100 µmol photons m⁻² s⁻¹, 5% CO₂ supply, and 30°C due to higher carboxylation efficiency of thermophilic rubisco variant
previews.protocolJson: [{"stepNumber":1,"title":"PCR Amplification of T. elongatus rbcL Gene","description":"Amplify rbcL gene from Thermosynechococcus elongatus BP-1 genomic DNA using high-fidelity polymerase with primers flanking the coding sequence. Verify product size by gel electrophoresis and purify using spin column kit.","duration":"3 hours","sourceProtocol":"Nature Protocols standard PCR protocol","sourceUrl":"https://www.nature.com/nprot/journal/v1/n5/full/nprot.2006.386.html","criticalNote":"Design primers to include restriction sites compatible with Ptrc vector backbone for downstream cloning"},{"stepNumber":2,"title":"Construct Ptrc-rbcL Expression Vector","description":"Clone purified rbcL PCR product into pDF-lac vector downstream of Ptrc promoter using restriction digest and ligation. Transform into E. coli DH5α and verify insert by Sanger sequencing.","duration":"4 days","sourceProtocol":"pDF-lac cloning protocol from PMC2860132","sourceUrl":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2860132/...","criticalNote":"Ensure Ptrc promoter orientation and rbcL reading frame are correct; verify no frame-shift mutations"},{"stepNumber":3,"title":"Transform Synechocystis PCC 6803 with Ptrc-rbcL Construct","description":"Transform wild-type Synechocystis PCC 6803 cells with Ptrc-rbcL plasmid via natural transformation using 5 µg DNA per 10⁸ cells under low-light conditions. Select transformants on BG-11 agar plates with appropriate antibiotic.","duration":"10 days","sourceProtocol":"Synechocystis transformation protocol from Scientific Reports","sourceUrl":"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-020-61100-4","criticalNote":"Maintain cells at 30°C under low light (20 µmol photons m⁻² s⁻¹) during selection to prevent photoinhibition"},{"stepNumber":4,"title":"PCR Screen and Segregate Transformants to Homoplasmy","description":"Screen colonies by colony PCR and passage positive clones through three rounds of single-colony isolation on selective media to achieve complete chromosome segregation. Confirm homoplasmy by PCR of genomic DNA.","duration":"21 days","sourceProtocol":"Synechocystis segregation protocol from Nature Protocols","sourceUrl":"https://www.nature.com/nprot/journal/v3/n11/full/nprot.2008.183.html","criticalNote":"Synechocystis contains multiple chromosome copies; complete segregation typically requires 3-4 rounds of selection"},{"stepNumber":5,"title":"Validate rbcL Expression by Western Blot and qPCR","description":"Culture wild-type and Ptrc-rbcL strains to OD730=0.6, harvest cells, and extract total protein and RNA. Perform western blot using anti-RbcL antibody and qPCR using rbcL-specific primers to quantify expression levels.","duration":"2 days","sourceProtocol":"Cyanobacteria protein extraction from Bio-protocol","sourceUrl":"https://bio-protocol.org/e1653","criticalNote":"Use 16S rRNA or rnpB as qPCR reference gene; confirm rubisco protein assembly by native PAGE if possible"},{"stepNumber":6,"title":"Culture Cells Under Experimental Conditions","description":"Grow wild-type and Ptrc-rbcL strains in BG-11 medium under continuous illumination at 100 µmol photons m⁻² s⁻¹, 5% CO₂, and 30°C with agitation. Monitor growth by OD730 daily and harvest at mid-log phase (OD730=0.5-0.8).","duration":"7 days","sourceProtocol":"Synechocystis cultivation protocol from protocols.io","sourceUrl":"https://protocols.io/view/cyanobacteria-cultivation-protocol-n2bvj6164gk5","criticalNote":"Maintain constant CO₂ bubbling at 5% and verify light intensity with quantum sensor at culture surface"},{"stepNumber":7,"title":"Measure Carbon Fixation by ¹⁴C-Bicarbonate Incorporation","description":"Incubate cells (OD730=0.6) with 1 µCi/mL NaH¹⁴CO₃ for 5 minutes under experimental conditions, quench with acid, collect cells on filters, and measure ¹⁴C incorporation by scintillation counting. Calculate fixation rate normalized to cell density.","duration":"4 hours","sourceProtocol":"¹⁴C-bicarbonate assay from JOVE protocols","sourceUrl":"https://www.jove.com/science-education/10398/measuring-photosynthesis","criticalNote":"Use three biological replicates per strain; normalize to chlorophyll content and cell number for accuracy"},{"stepNumber":8,"title":"Measure Oxygen Evolution Rate","description":"Measure photosynthetic O₂ evolution using Clark-type electrode under saturating light (200 µmol photons m⁻² s⁻¹) and 5% CO₂ at 30°C. Record steady-state O₂ production rate over 10 minutes and normalize to chlorophyll content.","duration":"3 hours","sourceProtocol":"Oxygen evolution protocol from Nature Scientific Reports","sourceUrl":"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-020-61100-4","criticalNote":"Calibrate electrode with air-saturated and nitrogen-purged buffer before each measurement session"}]

previews.validationJson: {"validationApproach":"The primary endpoint of at least 25% increase in carbon fixation rate will be validated using two complementary methods: ¹⁴C-bicarbonate incorporation assay and oxygen evolution measurements. Both assays will be performed on mid-log phase cultures (OD730=0.5-0.8) under the specified conditions (30°C, 100 µmol photons m⁻² s⁻¹, 5% CO₂) with three biological replicates per strain. Success requires both wild-type and Ptrc-rbcL strains to show reproducible fixation rates with Ptrc-rbcL demonstrating ≥25% higher rate normalized to cell density and chlorophyll content. Western blot confirmation of elevated RbcL protein levels ensures the phenotype correlates with target gene overexpression.","totalBudgetEstimate":3690,"currency":"GBP"}
